artifact.feature_name: Financial Telemetry Phase 1.4 — Validation: Lint Enforcement + Truth-Telling Tests
artifact.predecessor_phase: Phase 1.3 — UI Layer: Split Display + Labels (EXEC-077, closed 2026-05-04)

surfaces: eslint.config.mjs — 2 import lines + 2 scoped config blocks
__tests__/financial-surface/ or app/api/__tests__/ — 1 new Jest file, 1-2 representative routes
e2e/financial-enforcement.spec.ts — 1 new Playwright file: 2-3 DOM assertions + 2 I5 scenarios
coverage_mode.targeted_mode_justification: Lint rules activate globally — partial activation produces no meaningful protection for Enforcement. Runtime validation (API tests, Playwright) is intentionally limited to representative surfaces sufficient to prove invariants. Exhaustive surface Playwright coverage is a separate regression suite category and does not belong in an Enforcement FIB. Phase 1.4 is a CI guardrail, not a validation system.

layer_budget.secondary_layer_constraints: none — no service, API shape, UI component, or infrastructure layer receives logic changes
layer_budget.dto_classification: no DTO changes in this slice — service DTOs are stable from Phase 1.2B; the no-unlabeled-financial-value rule reads DTO definitions, it does not change them

scope_guardrail.one_line_boundary: This FIB introduces CI guardrails — minimal ESLint rules and targeted test assertions — that prevent reintroduction of unlabeled financial values, forbidden labels, and misleading completeness rendering. It does not introduce exhaustive surface validation or new enforcement infrastructure.

atomicity_test.atomicity_notes: After Phase 1.4 ships, CI is red on any new violation; the system is correctly enforced. lib/format.ts helper removal requires the lint guard this phase provides, not the reverse. I1-I4 harness begins from outbox DDL and consumer, not from lint rules.

adjacent_consequence_ledger.why_adjacent: Completing the formatters-consolidated-into-FinancialValue story; natural final step of the migration
adjacent_consequence_ledger.disposition: Phase 1.5 cleanup or named follow-on slice — removal requires lint guard first (which this FIB provides); breaking exports before the guard exists makes the migration order fragile

adjacent_consequence_ledger.temptation: Full I1-I4 failure harness activation (atomicity, crash recovery, duplicate delivery, replay)
adjacent_consequence_ledger.why_adjacent: Harness file is labeled EXEC-READY; activating the whole thing alongside I5 feels complete
adjacent_consequence_ledger.disposition: Wave 2 only — finance_outbox, outbox consumer, and projections do not exist in Wave 1; writing tests against stubs produces no production signal

adjacent_consequence_ledger.temptation: OpenAPI YAML lint rule (spectral, redocly, or custom YAML parser)
adjacent_consequence_ledger.why_adjacent: Logical complement to no-unlabeled-financial-value at the schema level
adjacent_consequence_ledger.disposition: Separate toolchain decision — not part of the ESLint flat config pattern; requires its own CI step, configuration file, and ruleset definition

adjacent_consequence_ledger.temptation: Runtime usage tracking / structured logs per deprecated-label or forbidden-label hit
adjacent_consequence_ledger.why_adjacent: Adjacent while wiring CI — 'add observability while we're here' pattern
adjacent_consequence_ledger.disposition: Observability class — GOV-FIB-001 §14 places Observability after Enforcement; explicitly deferred since Phase 1.2B-C

adjacent_consequence_ledger.why_adjacent: Questions surface naturally during Phase 1.4 review; last implementation phase before Rollout
adjacent_consequence_ledger.disposition: Post-Phase 1.5 — gated on Wave 1 sign-off; authoring in Phase 1.4 may conflict with operator feedback from Phase 1.5 walkthrough

adjacent_consequence_ledger.temptation: Full surface Playwright coverage (all 13 migrated families)
adjacent_consequence_ledger.why_adjacent: Would guarantee every surface is continuously validated after Phase 1.3
adjacent_consequence_ledger.disposition: Rejected — Phase 1.4 is an enforcement gate, not a full UI validation system; representative coverage (2-3 surfaces) is sufficient to prove the authority-label invariant while keeping CI fast and stable

phase_boundary.one_line_invariant: If a change authors an ESLint enforcement rule, adds a test:surface script, births API boundary envelope tests for representative financial routes, or authors Playwright DOM / I5 truth-telling assertions against already-migrated surfaces — and nothing else — it is in scope.
stop_conditions: API contract / OpenAPI shape changes required
UI component births or props changes required
full I1-I4 harness activation (finance_outbox not present)
hold_percent, average_bet, policy thresholds, or loyalty points flagged by lint rules
new authority classes or completeness status values introduced
prohibited: API contract / OpenAPI changes (complete — Phase 1.2B-C, EXEC-076)
lib/format.ts exported helper removal (Phase 1.5 or named follow-on — requires lint guard first)
Full I1-I4 failure harness (Wave 2 — finance_outbox not present)
average_bet, min_bet, max_bet, watchlistFloor, ctrThreshold flagged (WAVE-1-CLASSIFICATION-RULES §6)

intent.operator_problem: Pit bosses and floor supervisors see correctly authority-labeled financial values across PT-2 surfaces after Phase 1.3. Without automated enforcement, nothing prevents a future code change from reintroducing a bare currency number in a service DTO, adding a forbidden label back to a UI component, or rendering $0 or blank when completeness is partial or unknown. The operator would see a number that appears complete and authoritative but is silently misrepresenting incomplete data. No CI gate prevents violations from re-entering; no automated test verifies surfaces correctly communicate incomplete or uncomputed financial data.
intent.pilot_fit: Phase 1.4 delivers Wave 1 exit criteria 2 (lint rule red on violations, active in CI) and 3 (truth-telling test suite passes, harness I5 subset). Without both, the Wave 1 → Wave 2 handoff gate cannot be passed regardless of surface cleanliness. Phase 1.3 EXEC-077 explicitly deferred enforcement to Phase 1.4 in its Adjacent Consequence Ledger and Phase 1.4 handoff section.

entities.name: no-forbidden-financial-label.js
entities.description: New CJS ESLint rule in .eslint-rules/ implementing §4.1-4.5 from WAVE-1-FORBIDDEN-LABELS.md: Handle with derived-rename exception, Win with qualifier whitelist, Coverage KPI-context scoped to named file patterns and JSX elements, Theo:0/$0 patterns, DTO chip identifier patterns on services/**/dtos.ts files.
fields_hint: Sub-rules: §4.1 Handle, §4.2 Win, §4.3 Coverage, §4.4 Theo:0, §4.5 DTO chip identifiers

entities.name: no-unlabeled-financial-value.js
entities.description: New CJS ESLint rule in .eslint-rules/ with deliberately simple, explicit scope: in services/**/dtos.ts, flag explicitly identified financial fields in DTOs (fields previously migrated in Phase 1.3) that remain typed as number or number | null. The DTO sub-rule operates on a maintained allowlist of known financial DTO fields, not all numeric fields. In components/**/*.tsx and app/**/*.tsx, flag direct formatDollars(x.value) / formatDollars(field.value) call expressions where the argument is a member access on a FinancialValue-typed variable. No heuristic identifier-pattern inference.
fields_hint: Scope: services/**/dtos.ts for DTO sub-rule; components/**/*.tsx + app/**/*.tsx for render sub-rule

entities.name: eslint.config.mjs additions
entities.description: Two new import lines (top-level import block, matching existing pattern for 10 current rules) and two new scoped config blocks: one targeting components/**/*.{ts,tsx} and app/**/*.{ts,tsx} for no-forbidden-financial-label and the formatDollars render sub-rule of no-unlabeled-financial-value; one targeting services/**/dtos.ts for the DTO currency-field sub-rule and §4.5 chip-identifier sub-rule. Test file glob exclusions follow existing convention.
fields_hint: 2 scoped config blocks with plugins + rules entries

entities.name: test:surface script
entities.description: New package.json script following test:slice:* naming pattern. Runs jest with --testPathPatterns covering: components/financial/__tests__ (Phase 1.3 shared component tests), Phase 1.3 DEF-006 test dirs, and Phase 1.4-born API boundary envelope test file. Exact pattern TBD during EXEC-SPEC scaffolding.
fields_hint: Includes Phase 1.3 test targets (DEF-006, financial components)
Includes Phase 1.4 API boundary test file

entities.name: API route boundary envelope test file
entities.description: One new Jest test file targeting 1-2 representative financial routes sufficient to validate envelope shape at the API boundary. Each test asserts response body carries FinancialValue-shaped objects: integer value, non-null type, non-null source, non-null completeness.status at known financial field paths.
fields_hint: Assertions: response.body.field.value is integer, .type non-null, .source non-null, .completeness.status non-null

entities.name: financial-enforcement.spec.ts
entities.description: One new Playwright E2E test file combining minimal DOM authority assertions (2-3 representative surfaces) and the I5 truth-telling Wave 1 subset (2 scenarios). I5 Scenario 1: open visit → start-from-previous panel → assert Partial badge, no $0.00 for total_buy_in/total_cash_out/net. I5 Scenario 2: Theo-unknown player → player 360 summary-band → assert 'Not computed' or 'Unknown' near Theo row, no $0.00.
fields_hint: I5 Scenario 1: start-from-previous panel, partial completeness
I5 Scenario 2: player-360 summary-band, Theo unknown

boundaries: No service layer changes — Phase 1.2B complete
No API / OpenAPI contract changes — Phase 1.2B-C complete (EXEC-076)
No UI component births or props changes — Phase 1.3 complete (EXEC-077); FinancialValue.tsx props contract frozen
average_bet, min_bet, max_bet, watchlistFloor, ctrThreshold not flagged (WAVE-1-CLASSIFICATION-RULES §6)
Loyalty points not flagged (non-currency unit system)
lib/format.ts exported helpers unchanged — removal deferred to Phase 1.5
Full I1-I4 harness not activated — Wave 2 infrastructure does not exist

capabilities.name: no-forbidden-financial-label ESLint rule
capabilities.description: Author .eslint-rules/no-forbidden-financial-label.js following the existing CJS flat-config plugin pattern. Implement sub-rules §4.1-4.5 verbatim from WAVE-1-FORBIDDEN-LABELS.md. Global activation via eslint.config.mjs.
capabilities.output: CI exits non-zero on any forbidden label appearing in production UI or DTO files; exits 0 on clean post-Phase-1.3 codebase

capabilities.name: no-unlabeled-financial-value ESLint rule
capabilities.description: Author .eslint-rules/no-unlabeled-financial-value.js with explicit, reliable scope: DTO number-typed fields in services/**/dtos.ts and direct formatDollars member-access call expressions in component/app files. No heuristic inference. Activation via eslint.config.mjs.
capabilities.output: CI exits non-zero on allowlisted financial DTO fields that remain typed as number or number | null and bare formatter calls on FinancialValue-typed variables; exits 0 on clean codebase; rule scope is narrow enough to avoid false-positives on hold_percent, average_bet, policy thresholds

capabilities.name: eslint.config.mjs integration
capabilities.description: Import both new rules at the top of eslint.config.mjs following the established pattern for all 10 existing rules. Add two scoped config blocks with appropriate glob patterns. Test file exclusions follow existing convention.
capabilities.output: Both rules active in npm run lint; zero violations on clean post-Phase-1.3 codebase; test introduction of label: 'Handle' causes npm run lint to exit non-zero

capabilities.name: test:surface script birth

capabilities.output: npm run test:surface is a stable, invokable exit gate runner for the Phase 1.4 enforcement gate test set

capabilities.name: API route boundary envelope tests

Each test asserts the response body carries FinancialValue-shaped objects (integer value, non-null type, non-null source, non-null completeness.status) at known financial field paths. Representative coverage proves the contract holds at the wire without duplicating Phase 1.2B-C route matrices.
capabilities.output: Runtime contract validation layer; representative routes prove API surface contract holds post-Phase-1.3

capabilities.name: Playwright DOM assertions + I5 truth-telling tests
capabilities.description: Author e2e/financial-enforcement.spec.ts combining: (a) DOM authority label presence assertions on 2-3 representative surfaces (shift-dashboard-v3 + player-360); (b) I5 Scenario 1: open visit → start-from-previous panel → assert Partial badge, no $0.00; (c) I5 Scenario 2: Theo-unknown player → player-360 summary-band → assert 'Not computed'/'Unknown', no $0.00. All concerns in one file to minimize test infrastructure overhead.
capabilities.output: Authority label visibility proven on representative surfaces; I5 Wave 1 invariant verified — completeness never silently misrepresented as complete or zero

capabilities.name: Validation sequence + tracker update

Update ROLLOUT-TRACKER.json: advance cursor to active_phase 1.5, record Phase 1.4 exit date, EXEC reference, commit SHA.

where.primary_surface: CI pipeline — npm run lint and npm run test:surface; Playwright E2E suite for I5 truth-telling. PT-2 UI surfaces are tested targets, not modified artifacts.

surfaces.name: e2e/financial-enforcement.spec.ts
surfaces.change_type: birth — Playwright DOM + I5 truth-telling spec

inbound.name: pull_request_opened
inbound.description: Developer opens a PR touching financial DTO, UI component, or app route — lint runs on the diff

inbound.name: ci_test_run
inbound.description: CI invokes npm run test:surface and npm run e2e:playwright on every PR

outbound.name: ci_lint_failure
outbound.description: npm run lint exits non-zero — PR blocked at CI boundary on forbidden label or unlabeled DTO field

outbound.name: ci_test_failure
outbound.description: npm run test:surface or npm run e2e:playwright exits non-zero — PR blocked at CI boundary

bounded_contexts: cross-cutting enforcement (lint rules span all financial service boundaries)
financial-display (Playwright DOM assertions target migrated surfaces; API tests target financial routes)

must not author service, API, or DTO shape changes

actors.name: CI system
actors.role: Runs npm run lint, npm run test:surface, npm run e2e:playwright on every PR. Enforces Phase 1.4 gates without human gate-keeping.

actors.name: Pit boss / floor supervisor

trigger_events.description: Wave 1 exit criterion 2 ('Lint rule red on violations, active in CI') and criterion 3 ('Truth-telling test suite passes, harness I5 subset') cannot be met without Phase 1.4. Wave 1 → Wave 2 handoff gate blocked.

lifecycle_events: Both rules imported and scoped in eslint.config.mjs
API route boundary envelope test file authored and passing
financial-enforcement.spec.ts authored and passing (DOM assertions + I5 Scenario 1 + I5 Scenario 2)
Full validation sequence passes: npm run lint (0 violations), npm run test:surface (all pass), npm run e2e:playwright (all pass)
Phase 1.4 exit gate met — Phase 1.5 entry unblocked
timing_constraints: Must complete before Phase 1.5 Rollout & Sign-off can begin
Must complete before Wave 1 exit criteria 2 and 3 can be declared met

Playwright seed data must provide: (1) a player with an open visit (partial completeness scenario) and (2) a player with a Theo-unknown state (unknown completeness scenario) — EXEC-SPEC must verify or patch seed

business_rules.statement: no-forbidden-financial-label must catch all five forbidden-label patterns from WAVE-1-FORBIDDEN-LABELS.md §4.1-4.5: Handle (with derived-rename exception), Win (with qualifier whitelist), Coverage (KPI-context scoped), Theo:0/$0, DTO chip identifiers on services/**/dtos.ts files.

no-unlabeled-financial-value must operate only on a maintained allowlist of explicitly identified financial DTO fields in services/**/dtos.ts that remain typed as number or number | null, and explicit formatDollars member-access calls in component/app files. No heuristic identifier-pattern inference. The Playwright assertions cover what the rule cannot reach.

The no-unlabeled-financial-value rule must NOT flag hold_percent (dimensionless ratio), average_bet, policy thresholds (min_bet, max_bet, watchlistFloor, ctrThreshold), loyalty points, or current_segment_average_bet. These are operator inputs, ratios, or non-currency unit systems per WAVE-1-CLASSIFICATION-RULES §6.

test:surface script must include Phase 1.3 test targets (components/financial/__tests__, DEF-006 dirs) and Phase 1.4-born API boundary test file. It must be the single exit gate runner for the Phase 1.4 enforcement gate test set.

API route boundary envelope tests must assert that each tested route response carries FinancialValue-shaped objects: value is integer (not float), type is non-null, source is non-null, completeness.status is non-null. Representative coverage (1-2 routes) is sufficient; exhaustive route testing is not the goal.

I5 Scenario 1: Navigate to a player session with an open visit. Open the start-from-previous panel. Assert the DOM contains the partial-completeness badge text (Partial or equivalent). Assert no $0.00 or empty currency cell for total_buy_in, total_cash_out, or net.

I5 Scenario 2: Navigate to a player 360 profile where the Theo estimate is unknown. Assert the DOM contains 'Not computed' or 'Unknown' near the Theo row. Assert the Theo row does not contain $0.00 or bare zero.
ROLLOUT-TRACKER.json must be updated with Phase 1.4 closure: cursor advanced to active_phase 1.5, exit date recorded, EXEC reference and commit SHA recorded. ROLLOUT-PROGRESS.md must be kept in sync.

invariants: CI exits non-zero on any new forbidden label (Handle, unqualified Win, Coverage quality, Theo:0/$0, totalChipsOut/totalChipsIn) in production UI or DTO files
CI exits non-zero on any allowlisted financial DTO field in services/**/dtos.ts that remains typed as number or number | null instead of FinancialValue
npm run test:surface passes on clean post-Phase-1.3 codebase
Playwright assertions confirm authority labels are visible in DOM on representative surfaces
Completeness is never silently misrepresented — I5 invariant: partial renders Partial badge, unknown renders Not computed, neither renders $0.00 or blank
hold_percent is never FinancialValue — lint rule must not flag it (DEF-NEVER, all phases)
No service, API, DTO shape, or UI component changes in this phase — all upstream contracts are stable
decision_notes: Coverage mode is Targeted (representative enforcement), not Full. Lint rules are global by nature and provide comprehensive protection. Runtime tests (API, Playwright) prove invariants on representative surfaces — exhaustive coverage is a regression suite, not an Enforcement FIB.

no-unlabeled-financial-value is intentionally simple: explicit DTO definition detection + explicit member-access formatDollars call detection. Heuristic identifier-pattern inference (e.g., *Cents, *Amount suffixes) was rejected as unreliable and likely to produce false-positives on non-currency fields with similar naming patterns.

I5 Wave 1 scope is Playwright DOM navigation only — no FailureFlags injection, no crash simulation, no finance_outbox interaction. Full I1-I4 harness is Wave 2. Two I5 scenarios prove the specific completeness rendering invariant that Phase 1.3 established.

Whether API route boundary envelope tests run as Jest/JSDOM simulations or against a live local Supabase instance is an open question for EXEC-SPEC to decide based on CI cost and route handler testability. Both modes are acceptable; the FIB requires the tests exist and pass.

Playwright seed data verification is an open question for EXEC-SPEC: must confirm local seed provides a player with an open visit (I5 Scenario 1) and a player with a Theo-unknown state (I5 Scenario 2). If seed gaps exist, EXEC-SPEC must include a seed data patch as a WS0 prerequisite.
